issues: Cache bypass (DisableFor) configured for corev1.Secret. This is a common fix for OOM caused by informer cache flooding from high-cardinality types (e.g., opendatahub-io/model-registry-operator#457)
No DefaultTransform: managedFields cached for all objects (wasted memory). Add cache.DefaultTransform to strip managedFields and reduce memory footprint
No GOMEMLIMIT set in deployment (Go GC cannot pressure-tune). Set GOMEMLIMIT to 80-90% of container memory limit for optimal GC behavior
Type ModelRegistry is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)

fields.name: Mode
fields.doc: The value of this field determines how TLS is enforced. SIMPLE: Secure connections with standard TLS semantics. In this mode client certificate is not requested during handshake. MUTUAL: Secure connections to the downstream using mutual TLS by presenting server certificates for authentication. A client certificate will also be requested during the handshake and at least one valid certificate is required to be sent by the client. ISTIO_MUTUAL: Secure connections from the downstream using mutual TLS by presenting server certificates for authentication. Compared to Mutual mode, this mode uses certificates, representing gateway workload identity, generated automatically by Istio for mTLS authentication. When this mode is used, all other TLS fields should be empty. OPTIONAL_MUTUAL: Similar to MUTUAL mode, except that the client certificate is optional. Unlike SIMPLE mode, A client certificate will still be explicitly requested during handshake, but the client is not required to send a certificate. If a client certificate is presented, it will be validated. ca_certificates should be specified for validating client certificates.

fields.name: CredentialName
fields.doc: The name of the secret that holds the TLS certs including the CA certificates. If not provided, it is set automatically using model registry operator env variable DEFAULT_CERT. An Opaque secret should contain the following keys and values: `tls.key: <privateKey>` and `tls.crt: <serverCert>` or `key: <privateKey>` and `cert: <serverCert>`. For mutual TLS, `cacert: <CACertificate>` and `crl: <CertificateRevocationList>` can be provided in the same secret or a separate secret named `<secret>-cacert`. A TLS secret for server certificates with an additional `tls.ocsp-staple` key for specifying OCSP staple information, `ca.crt` key for CA certificates and `ca.crl` for certificate revocation list is also supported. Only one of server certificates and CA certificate or credentialName can be specified.

fields.name: Port
fields.doc: Listen port for server connections, defaults to 80 without TLS and 443 when TLS settings are present.

fields.name: TLS
fields.doc: Set of TLS related options that govern the server's behavior. Use these options to control if all http requests should be redirected to https, and the TLS modes to use.

fields.name: GatewayRoute
fields.doc: Creates an OpenShift Route for Gateway Service when set to enabled (default).

fields.name: Domain
fields.doc: Domain name for Gateway configuration. Must follow DNS952 subdomain conventions. If not provided, it is set automatically using model registry operator env variable DEFAULT_DOMAIN. If the env variable is not set, it is set to the OpenShift `cluster` ingress domain in an OpenShift cluster.

fields.name: IstioIngress
fields.doc: Value of label `istio` used to identify the Ingress Gateway

fields.name: ControlPlane
fields.doc: Maistra/OpenShift Servicemesh control plane name

fields.name: Rest
fields.doc: Rest gateway server config

fields.name: Grpc
fields.doc: gRPC  gateway server config

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Kubernetes configmap name

fields.name: Key
fields.doc: Key name in configmap

fields.name: AuthProvider
fields.doc: Authorino authentication provider name If missing, it is set using the operator environment property DEFAULT_AUTH_PROVIDER Model registry will have an error status if the operator property is also missing

fields.name: AuthConfigLabels
fields.doc: Authorino AuthConfig selector labels. If missing, it is set using the operator environment property DEFAULT_AUTH_CONFIG_LABELS

fields.name: TlsMode
fields.doc: DestinationRule TLS mode. Defaults to ISTIO_MUTUAL. DISABLE: Do not setup a TLS connection to the upstream endpoint. SIMPLE: Originate a TLS connection to the upstream endpoint. MUTUAL: Secure connections to the upstream using mutual TLS by presenting client certificates for authentication. ISTIO_MUTUAL: Secure connections to the upstream using mutual TLS by presenting client certificates for authentication. Compared to Mutual mode, this mode uses certificates generated automatically by Istio for mTLS authentication. When this mode is used, all other fields in `ClientTLSSettings` should be empty.

fields.name: Gateway
fields.doc: Optional Istio Gateway for registry services. Gateway is not created if set to null (default).

fields.name: Audiences
fields.doc: Optional Authorino AuthConfig credential audiences. This depends on the cluster identity provider. If not specified, operator will determine the cluster's audience using its own service account.

api_types.name: OAuthProxyConfig

fields.name: Port
fields.doc: Listen port for OAuth Proxy connections, defaults to 8443

fields.name: TLSCertificateSecret
fields.doc: This parameter specifies the Kubernetes Secret name and key of the proxy public key certificate. If this option is not set, the operator uses OpenShift Serving Certificate by default in OpenShift clusters. In non-OpenShift clusters, a secret named `<modelregistry-name>-oauth-proxy` MUST be provided with data keys `tls.crt` and `tls.key` for the certificate and secret key respectively.

fields.name: TLSKeySecret
fields.doc: This parameter specifies the optional Kubernetes Secret name and key used for the proxy private key if `SSLCertificateSecret` is set.

fields.name: ServiceRoute
fields.doc: Create an OpenShift Route for REST proxy Service, enabled by default

fields.name: Domain
fields.doc: Domain name for Route configuration. Must follow DNS952 subdomain conventions. If not provided, it is set automatically using model registry operator env variable DEFAULT_DOMAIN. If the env variable is not set, it is set to the OpenShift `cluster` ingress domain in an OpenShift cluster.

fields.name: RoutePort
fields.doc: Listen port for OAuth Proxy Route connections, defaults to 443 in OpenShift router default configuration

fields.name: Image
fields.doc: Optional image to support overriding the image deployed by the operator.

fields.doc: True if skipping database instance creation during ML Metadata service initialization. By default, it is false.

fields.name: SSLCertificateSecret

fields.name: SSLKeySecret

fields.name: SSLPasswordSecret
fields.doc: This parameter specifies the Kubernetes Secret name and key of the password for the SSL secret key specified in sslKeySecret, allowing client certificate private keys to be stored in encrypted form on disk even when interactive passphrase input is not practical.

fields.name: Host
fields.doc: The hostname or IP address of the MYSQL server: If unspecified, a connection to the local host is assumed. Currently, a replicated MYSQL backend is not supported.

fields.name: Port
fields.doc: Port number to connect to at the server host. The TCP Port number that the MYSQL server accepts connections on. If unspecified, the default MYSQL port (3306) is used.

fields.name: Username
fields.doc: The MYSQL login id.

fields.name: PasswordSecret
fields.doc: The password to use for `Username`. If empty, only MYSQL user ids that don't have a password set are allowed to connect.

fields.name: Database
fields.doc: The database to connect to. Must be specified. After connecting to the MYSQL server, this database is created if not already present unless SkipDBCreation is set. All queries after Connect() are assumed to be for this database.

fields.name: SkipDBCreation
fields.doc: True if skipping database instance creation during ML Metadata service initialization. By default, it is false.

fields.name: SSLCertificateSecret
fields.doc: This parameter specifies the Kubernetes Secret name and key of the client public key certificate.

fields.name: SSLKeySecret
fields.doc: This parameter specifies the Kubernetes Secret name and key used for the client private key.

fields.name: SSLRootCertificateConfigMap
fields.doc: This parameter specifies the Kubernetes ConfigMap name and key containing certificate authority (CA) certificate.

fields.name: SSLRootCertificatesConfigMapName
fields.doc: This parameter specifies the Kubernetes ConfigMap name containing multiple certificate authority (CA) certificate(s) as keys.

fields.name: SSLRootCertificateSecret
fields.doc: This parameter specifies the Kubernetes Secret name and key containing certificate authority (CA) certificate.

fields.name: SSLRootCertificatesSecretName
fields.doc: This parameter specifies the Kubernetes Secret name containing multiple certificate authority (CA) certificate(s) as keys.

fields.name: SSLCipher
fields.doc: This parameter specifies the list of permissible ciphers for SSL encryption.

fields.name: VerifyServerCert
fields.doc: If set, enable verification of the server certificate against the host name used when connecting to the server.

api_types.name: PostgresConfig

fields.name: Host
fields.doc: Name of host to connect to.

fields.name: HostAddress
fields.doc: Numeric IP address of host to connect to. If this field is provided, "host" field is ignored.

fields.name: Port
fields.doc: Port number to connect to at the server host.

fields.name: Username
fields.doc: PostgreSQL username to connect as.

fields.name: PasswordSecret
fields.doc: Password to be used if required by the PostgreSQL server.

fields.name: Database
fields.doc: The database name.

fields.name: SkipDBCreation
fields.doc: True if skipping database instance creation during Model Registry service initialization. By default, it is false.

fields.name: GenerateDeployment
fields.doc: Auto-provision a PostgreSQL database if true.

fields.name: SSLMode
fields.doc: PostgreSQL sslmode setup. Values can be disable, allow, prefer, require, verify-ca, verify-full.

fields.name: SSLCertificateSecret
fields.doc: This parameter specifies the Kubernetes Secret name and key of the client SSL certificate.

fields.name: SSLKeySecret
fields.doc: This parameter specifies the Kubernetes Secret name and key used for the client certificate SSL secret key.

fields.name: SSLPasswordSecret
fields.doc: This parameter specifies the Kubernetes Secret name and key of the password for the SSL secret key specified in sslKeySecret, allowing client certificate private keys to be stored in encrypted form on disk even when interactive passphrase input is not practical.

fields.name: SSLRootCertificateConfigMap
fields.doc: This parameter specifies the Kubernetes ConfigMap name and key containing SSL certificate authority (CA) certificate(s).

fields.name: SSLRootCertificateSecret
fields.doc: This parameter specifies the Kubernetes Secret name and key containing SSL certificate authority (CA) certificate(s).

fields.name: Image
fields.doc: Optional image to support overriding the PostgreSQL image deployed by the operator. If not specified, defaults to the operator's configured PostgreSQL image.

markers: +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="has(self.generateDeployment) && self.generateDeployment ? true : (size(self.host) > 0 || size(self.hostAddress) > 0)",message="GenerateDeployment must be set to true if host/hostAddress are not provided"
+kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="has(self.database) && size(self.database) > 0 ? has(self.username) && size(self.username) > 0 : true",message="Username must be set if database is specified"

+kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="!(has(self.generateDeployment) && self.generateDeployment) || ((!has(self.host) || size(self.host) == 0) && (!has(self.hostAddress) || size(self.hostAddress) == 0))",message="host and hostAddress must not be set when generateDeployment is true"

operator_config.name: dnsLabelRegex
operator_config.doc:
dnsLabelRegex matches valid Kubernetes DNS label names (RFC 1123).
ConfigMap names are DNS subdomains (dots allowed) but volume names must be DNS labels (no dots).

operator_config.name: deploymentDelay
operator_config.doc: deploymentDelay is how long to wait after the deployment is available to consider it ready.

operator_config.name: errRegexp
operator_config.doc:
errRegexp is based on the CHECK_EQ macro output used by REST container.
For more details on Abseil logging and CHECK_EQ macro see [Abseil documentation].

[Abseil documentation]: https://abseil.io/docs/cpp/guides/logging#CHECK

summary: model-registry-operator is a Kubernetes component. managing 1 CRD(s): ModelRegistry. deploying 1 workload(s) (model-registry-operator-controller-manager) with 1 container(s). watching 11 resource type(s). exposing 6 service(s). with 2 webhook(s) (1 validating, 1 mutating). protected by 4 network policy/policies. defining 6 API spec type(s) across 27 struct(s) with 152 total fields. with 39 operator config constants (12 image references). reporting 9 status condition types. with 1 platform-conditional resource paths. with 1 ingress route(s).
